## [00:00 - 03:02]

Hey everybody, David O'Connell here, and today we're going to be talking about Google demand generation campaigns. This includes both display and video ad formats. During this module, we're going to cover what demand generation ads are. We're going to talk about where they are served, and we're going to talk about how to make effective demand generation ads. I think the first and most important thing to know about demand generation ads is that this is where the volume is for direct response advertisers. When you hear about advertisers using Google ads to acquire hundreds of customers a day using cold traffic, it's usually going to be through demand generation campaigns at this point in time. That wasn't the case. Up until earlier this year, the big campaign types to drive mass customer acquisition from cold traffic were really display and YouTube campaigns. But Google made a big change earlier this year, and they said, hey, if you're going to run any video campaigns for which you want to optimize for a conversion action, which is basically every direct response campaign you're ever going to run, you now have to run those video campaigns as demand generation campaigns in order to use the conversion optimization functions. Since conversion optimization is such a big part of success, all YouTube campaigns for direct response advertisers are now moving into demand generation campaigns. Something similar has happened with display ads because also in April of this year, April of 2025, Google has also allowed demand generation campaigns to expand into all the Google display network inventory. So now you can take campaigns that have already learned within Google's properties and you can expand them into the Google display network and get a huge extension on your reach because the Google display network reaches over 90% of internet users worldwide. These two things have made a big change. That's why for DR advertisers now, demand generation is really is the place to be. I think it's the most important campaign type to be familiar with if you want to do mass customer acquisition on Google. So let's get into it here. So first, as always, we want to start with the context of where will the ads be shown, who will see the ads, and what is the ad format. So where do the ads show? These ads are going to show up across YouTube, whether it's video or image can show up on YouTube ads can show up in Gmail ads can show up in discover, which is very prevalent on Android devices. It's part of the native experience for an Android device or demand gen ads can show up

## [03:02 - 06:02]

anywhere across the Google display network. Who's going to see these ads? The ads here are going to be seen by whoever you target. Typically, these are going to be top of funnel, solution aware or problem aware buyers. You're really going for cold traffic here. Some of the audience targeting types that we often see do well are in-market audiences, which is a behavioral audience. Google can tell you based on all their data of where people are searching and what videos they're watching, are these people in the market to buy something. So you might, for example, have fitness buyers. You can also create custom segments. So you can create audiences of people who may have searched a specific keyword. So if you sell sunglasses and you want to get your ads in front of people who have recently been searching sunglasses, then you can get your ads on YouTube, Gmail, Discover, Google display network in front of those people. You can create customer lookalike segments by uploading a list of your customers. Or using your pixel data. You can do placement targeting. So you can say, hey, I want to be on these specific websites or I want to be on these specific channels. This is really good for contextual targeting. So again, if you're selling workout plans, you might want to be on bodybuilding websites and showing up on bodybuilding YouTube channels. Or finally, you can do demographic targeting. So we can go after men or women of certain ages. I will say something to keep in mind when you're doing demographic targeting. It can be very effective. But also there are very large categories of people that still show up in the unknown category on Google because a large percentage of Google users are not logged in. So Google doesn't have all the demographic information always. They tend to have a lot more of the behavioral information and the content information because that doesn't use personally identified information. And most demand gen traffic is going to be from mobile devices. And then finally, what are the ad formats here? So demand gen ads are going to be either image or video. Over time, you can develop demand gen campaigns that serve both. I advise starting with one. I'll talk a little bit more about that in this module. So you're going to have images or videos. And you're going to have them in multiple dimensions, multiple different sizes so that you can access all the different inventory that's available on the network. And then you're also going to have 40 character text headlines. You can have up to five of those. And then you can also have 90 character text descriptions, which you can also have up to five. And then Google will mix and match these things together based on what combinations Google

believes will perform best and then what Google measures performs best. Highly recommended to really only load headlines and descriptions that you believe are going to perform well. ## [09:03 - 12:04]

are you going to go after. As I said, mostly you want to go after cold traffic because that's where the volume is. You could use demand gen ads and create, for example, a retargeting audience. And if you did that, you're going to want to create ads that speak to highly aware audience. But usually we're going to be going after targeting options here where we want to reach a solution or problem-aware audience. So first decision you're going to make, the next decision you make after you choose your targeting is going to be to decide whether to lead with video or image assets. Usually you're going to want to have both, but I think it's very hard for advertisers to make great video assets and great image assets and optimize them all and have enough capital for testing all of them at the same time. So usually if you're just getting started with demand gen ads, encourage you to pick one, whichever one you tend to have stronger capabilities in, focus on getting some successful ads with that ad type. And then once you've made progress there, switch over to the other ad type to get that. If you have successful video ads, both video and image ads have access to a lot of inventory on Google. So if you can make effective ads in either format, you're going to be able to get a lot of volume. And when you really want to maximize your volume, that's when you're going to want to make sure you're running both image and video ads. Next point here, you have to interrupt. This is, I think, probably the most important thing when it comes to running demand gen ads. These are not people who are searching for you. These are not people who are saying they want to buy. These are people that are doing other things, they're going through their email or they're surfing the web or they're looking for videos on YouTube or they're watching videos on YouTube. And you have to stop them in their tracks with your image or your video hook or your headline. You have to stop them from scrolling. You have to grab their attention and be disruptive. So this really takes being very bright, standing out, finding ways to disrupt yourself and finding ways to disrupt and stand out. And we're going to talk about some examples. Something that's worth noting too as you get involved with Dimagine ads, you're going to if you develop video ads, I'm not going to go into a lot of detail here about creating great video ads. There's other modules in this course and there's lots of content about how to create great YouTube video ads. So assuming you've created great YouTube video ads and you upload them into your demand gen campaigns, what I want you to be aware of is that there's a lot of in-feed inventory, for example, on Discover or in the YouTube feed, which doesn't autoplay. So it's really important to make sure that you have great thumbnails.

## [12:04 - 15:05]

Otherwise, people may never click on your video and it doesn't matter how good your video hook is if they never see it. So that's a really, I think, important tip is if you're going to run video, make sure you pay a lot of attention to your thumbnails to maximize your performance across the Google network. I also recommend if you're using images to test using big text in your images. We usually see better results when we have big text in the images, but I do want to warn everybody that there are some websites in the Google Display Network that will not allow images that have text in them. So make sure you have some without text, make sure you have some with text. The ones with text will usually get you better performance. And then by having images without text, you're going to get more reach that way. I advise testing both and then going where the data shows you get the best performance. Also for images, you want to make sure you upload multiple asset sizes. So you want to make sure you have your square video, your widescreen video, your tall video, or excuse me, square image, widescreen image. You want to make sure you have all the different image types and all the video types that Google offers you. By doing this, you'll be able to show up in more inventory because Google will serve you where you have the right size. As I mentioned before, aim to fill all five headline and description slots. And then aim to use curiosity or specificity headlines. You really don't want to just come in here and say things like try our plan. You may want to say something like stop yo-yo dieting for good. I think as we go through some of the example ads next, you'll be able to see some examples of headlines that would perform well in demand gen. And then finally, like all ad types, you want to just always be testing, analyzing, and iterating on your top performing ads. And I think that matters more than ever in demand gen because of the high scale that's possible. A lot of times you can see advertisers come in here and they have a launch and it may be lukewarm at first. And then you do some more testing and you iterate on your top performing ads. And then all of a sudden you can find an ad that gets really low CPAs. And once you have that, then the volume can really explode. And this can really lead to the hockey stick kind of growth for a lot of companies. So really, really important here. Always be testing, analyzing.
